free_text: Interaction of 5-50 MeV/nucleon 3He and 4He with 59Co
For the in-beam gamma-ray measurements, a self-
supporting 5-10 mg/cm2 Co target was used.
For the off-line radioactivity measurements, target
assemblies consisted of a stack of Co foils (each
about 5-6 mg/cm2 thick) and Al forward and backword
catchers interspersed with beam energy degraders.
Data taken from article Tables I, II and III.
Approved by H.J.Karwowski, 89/4/14.
Updated, converted to lower case.
Data sorted.
Reference date corrected.
A self-supporting 5-10 mg/cm2 Co target was used.
In-beam gamma ray measurements.
Uncertainties originate mainly from
ambiguities in assigning gamma lines to specific
nuclides and, for low intensity peaks, in their area
determination.
Target assemblies consisted of a stack of Co foils
(each about 5-6 mg/cm2 thick) and Al forward and
backward catchers interspersed with beam energy
degraders.
Not analysed in detail.
